channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth and consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump has threatened tariffs on semiconductors, arguing that production must be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon's America: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: We're getting achingly closer to SOT-MRAM; the memory that could one day replace DRAM and NAND, but I can't see it happening soon
item.description: Researchers claim to have reached a goal that will allow SOT-MRAM to become reality sooner, just not too soon.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How cyberscams are draining Americans wallets by the billions
item.description: Scams cost Americans an estimated $159 billion every year and average about $3,500 in losses per victim. The CyberGuy has advice to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hop in, no driver needed for this future ride-hailing robotaxi
item.description: Zeekr and Waymo are joining forces for the world's first mass-produced autonomous vehicle, combining an electric vehicle with self-driving technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: She helped create the U.S. Digital Service. Now it’s become the U.S. DOGE Service
item.description: As former U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer, Jen Pahlka helped to create the U.S. Digital Service (USDS). Today, the USDS houses Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), hell-bent on quick, disruptive change.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: For February’s Patch Tuesday, Microsoft rolls out 63 updates
item.description: Microsoft released 63 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office, and developer platforms in this week’s Patch Tuesday update. The February release was a relatively light update, but it comes with significant testing requirements for networking and remote desktop environments.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk’s war on USAID is a war on reality
item.description: On January 29, President Donald Trump celebrated the latest victory in unelected billionaire Elon Musk’s crusade against “inefficiency” in the government: stopping $50 million from, according to Trump, “being sent to Gaza to buy condoms for Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia, look away! OpenAI is almost ready to deliver first prototype of its AI GPU - General Processing Unit
item.description: OpenAI has partnered with Broadcom to design its own AI chip in a bid for some independence from Nvidia, and it's nearly ready.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia forges healthcare partnerships to advance AI-driven genomics, drug discovery and more
item.description: Healthcare has the most significant upside, particularly in patient care, among all the industries applicable to AI. The healthcare industry creates massive amounts of data, but much of it lives in silos.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Zacks Investment hit in data breach - 12 million users potentially at risk
item.description: The company hasn't commented on the allegations yet, but users may wish to up their security protection.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Mummies' Surprising Smell Revealed by Science - A Study
item.description: Researchers from University College London's Institute for Sustainable Heritage discovered that well-preserved Egyptian mummies have a pleasant smell, contrary to popular depictions in media.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: TikTok returned to the Apple and Google app stores Thursday evening after President Trump delayed the ban on the video-sharing app last month. The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: SpaceX’s Starbase site inches closer to being its own city in Texas
item.description: A Texas county on Wednesday approved holding an election sought by SpaceX that would let residents living around billionaire Elon Musk’s company decide whether to formally create a new city called Starbase.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: 14 states sue Musk and Trump, calling billionaire's role unconstitutional
item.description: Fourteen states sued Elon Musk, his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) and President Trump on Thursday, arguing that the tech billionaire’s sweeping efforts to cut government spending are unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Those workplace communication tools you hate might actually be good for you
item.description: Many things irk people about the way modern companies operate. Workplace communication tools and so-called enterprise social media platforms are among the low-stakes but high-impact bugbears.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Screenshot-scanning malware discovered on Apple App Store in first-of-its-kind attack
item.description: Security experts have discovered that hackers are targeting apps on the App Store to spread malware that steals information from saved screenshots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth the remains of a Roman basilica on the site of a new London skyscraper
item.description: Work to give London yet another skyscraper has uncovered traces of the city’s origins almost 2,000 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 4 TikTok beauty trends you can try for yourself
item.description: Ken Maccarone joins "Morning in America" with a look at the top trending products from the editors at BestReviews.
item.dc:creator: Ken Maccarone, NewsNation

item.title: AI chatbots outperform doctors in diagnosing patients, study finds
item.description: Chatbots quickly surpassed human physicians in diagnostic reasoning — the crucial first step in clinical care — according to a new study published in the journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: EU pulls back – for the moment – on privacy and genAI liability compliance regulations
item.description: When the EU on Tuesday said it was not, at this time, moving ahead with critical legislation involving privacy and genAI liability issues, it honestly reported that members couldn’t agree. But the reasons why they couldn’t agree get much more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top translation apps for travelers
item.description: Travel abroad worry-free with these top translation apps for travelers. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson highlights some favorites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World
